label: Evaluation-driven development & release gates

node.label: Evaluation-driven development & release gates
node.definition: Evaluation-driven development makes tests, risk thresholds, regression suites, and release gates part of the iterative model and application lifecycle.
node.why_it_matters: Evaluation-driven development uses test evidence, risk thresholds, and regression results to decide whether a model or AI application should advance, roll back, or require more review.
limitations: Release gates can miss novel failures when the test suite, threat model, or monitored metric does not cover the deployment context.

dependencies.rationale: Evaluation-driven development uses benchmarks, tests, and task metrics as evidence for release or rollback decisions.
Evaluation-driven release gates use red-team and threat-model findings to decide whether residual risk is acceptable.
Evaluation-driven development can include human evaluation and preference studies when automated metrics do not capture quality or risk.
Evaluation-driven release processes use reproducibility checks, drift monitoring, and operational signals after deployment.
